tasks.finalAnswer:
## Results for May 2025

| Metric | Count |
|---|---|
| **Payment intents created** | **16** |
| **Payment attempts created** | **23** |

### Why the numbers differ

A **payment intent** represents one business payment goal for an order. A **payment attempt** is an individual request sent to a payment channel (PSP). The two numbers differ because **one intent can spawn multiple attempts** — if the first attempt fails (e.g., card declined, timeout), the system retries on another channel, creating additional attempts under the same intent.

In May 2025, the 16 intents collectively required 23 attempts, meaning **7 extra attempts** were retries of initially failed attempts. This is why intent success rate and attempt success rate are measured separately — an intent may eventually succeed even if its first attempt failed.

tasks.prompt: Of the payment intents created in April 2025, how many succeeded on their first attempt, and how many succeeded in the end (on any attempt)?

tasks.finalAnswer:
Here are the results for payment intents created in April 2025:

| Metric | Count |
|---|---|
| **Total payment intents created** | 15 |
| **Succeeded on first attempt** | **6** (intents 46, 47, 51, 54, 56, 60) |
| **Succeeded eventually (any attempt)** | **13** (the 6 above plus intents 48, 50, 52, 53, 57, 58, 59) |
| **Still processing** | 2 (intents 49, 55) |

So **6 intents** succeeded on their very first attempt, while **13 intents** ultimately succeeded after one or more retries. The remaining 2 intents are still in a "processing" state.

tasks.prompt: How many payments failed on their first attempt but eventually succeeded after switching to another channel? Count succeeded attempts whose attempt number is greater than 1.

tasks.finalAnswer:
**Answer: 33**

There are **33** succeeded attempts with `attempt_no > 1` whose first attempt (attempt 1) failed and the succeeding attempt switched to a different channel. Every one of these 33 retries used a different channel than the initial failed attempt.

content.text: [{"description":"Currencies; minor_unit_scale is the exponent between the minor unit stored in *_minor columns and the display unit (2 means 100 minor = 1 unit, 0 means minor = unit)","name":"currencies"},{"description":"Countries and their sales region","name":"countries"},{"description":"Tenant organizations (business owners of applications)","name":"organizations"},{"description":"Applications (independently operated products under an organization); currency_code is the settlement currency of all money amounts of that application","name":"applications"},{"description":"Platform login accounts (staff and operators); NOT the transacting customers — see the customers entity","name":"users"},{"description":"Transacting customers of an organization; deleted_at marks logical deletion (excluded from active-customer counts)","name":"customers"},{"description":"Sellable products; current price lives in product_price_versions (never on this table)","name":"products"},{"description":"Versioned product prices in minor units; a version is valid for order dates in [valid_from, valid_to), open-ended when valid_to is NULL. Historical orders keep the price valid at order creation","name":"product_price_versions"},{"description":"Business orders (transaction intent, NOT payment success); money in minor units of currency_code. created_at / confirmed_at / cancelled_at / completed_at are distinct lifecycle times","name":"orders"},{"description":"Order line items (one row per product in an order — finer grain than orders); unit_price_minor is the historical price at order creation","name":"order_items"},{"description":"External payment channels (PSPs)","name":"payment_channels"},{"description":"Versioned routing rules; a version applies to attempts whose created time falls in [valid_from, valid_to). Intent success rate and attempt success rate are different metrics","name":"payment_intents"},{"description":"Individual payment attempts: one intent can have several (failed attempts precede a retry on another channel). First-attempt result and final intent result differ","name":"payment_attempts"},{"description":"Routing decision per attempt: candidates, selected channel, the rule version in force at attempt time, and the reason","name":"routing_decisions"},{"description":"Refunds against succeeded payment intents; partial refunds are common (amount below the intent amount)","name":"refunds"},{"description":"Raw channel callback events: duplicates, delays, and out-of-order delivery all occur. external_event_id identifies the channel-side event; rows sharing it are duplicate deliveries. occurred_at (channel), received_at (gateway), processed_at (internal, NULL = received but not processed) are distinct times","name":"channel_callbacks"},{"description":"Internal ledger accounts (platform revenue, channel fee expense, merchant payable pools)","name":"ledger_accounts"},{"description":"Versioned channel fee rules in basis points; a version applies to payments whose success time falls in [valid_from, valid_to). Recomputing history must use the version valid at payment time","name":"fee_rule_versions"},{"description":"Ledger transactions (immutable posting units); entries of one transaction share its ID and balance to zero. A succeeded payment without a posted transaction has not reached the ledger yet","name":"ledger_transactions"},{"description":"Ledger entries: one debit/credit per row in minor units; direction is debit or credit; a transaction's entries sum to zero per currency","name":"ledger_entries"},{"description":"Settlements paid out to organizations; net_minor = gross - fees - refunds for the covered period. Settled is distinct from posted and from bank arrival","name":"settlements"},{"description":"Line items from channel-issued statements (external side of reconciliation); amounts and references come from the channel and can disagree with internal records","name":"external_statement_items"},{"description":"Reconciliation outcome per succeeded payment: matched, amount_mismatch, missing_external (internal success without a statement line), or missing_internal (statement line without internal record)","name":"reconciliation_results"},{"description":"Month-end account balance snapshots in minor units (a snapshot is a point-in-time fact, not a flow; recomputing from ledger_entries must agree)","name":"balance_snapshots"},{"description":"Creator agencies (guilds) taking a revenue share from signed creators","name":"agencies"},{"description":"Live streamers; agency_id NULL means independent (no agency share)","name":"creators"},{"description":"Long-lived live rooms (a room hosts many sessions; room lifetime income != single-session income)","name":"live_rooms"},{"description":"Individual broadcast sessions of a room (session grain is finer than room grain)","name":"live_sessions"},{"description":"Gift catalog; star prices are versioned in gift_price_versions (historical events keep the price at event time)","name":"gift_definitions"},{"description":"Versioned gift prices in stars (virtual asset, not fiat); valid for event times in [valid_from, valid_to), NULL valid_to = current","name":"gift_price_versions"},{"description":"Versioned revenue share rules in basis points; splits must use the version valid at gift event time","name":"revenue_share_rule_versions"},{"description":"Gift-sending events in sessions; NOT 1:1 with charges — charge_failed events consumed nothing, reversed events were charged then reversed, duplicate rows (dedup_of set) are repeat deliveries and must not be double counted","name":"gift_events"},{"description":"Revenue split per processed gift event in stars, using the share rule version valid at event time; platform_stars = total - creator - agency","name":"revenue_splits"},{"description":"Completed creator settlements in stars for a covered period; creator income after the period end is pending and NOT included","name":"creator_settlements"},{"description":"Customers visible to the current tenant only; tasks.finalAnswer:
Now I can analyze the data. Let me summarize the results:

**Total callback rows:** 147

**Distinct external_event_ids:** I can see from the grouped results:
- 14 external_event_ids appear 2 times each (duplicates)
- The rest appear 1 time each

Let me calculate: 
- 14 IDs × 2 rows = 28 rows from duplicated IDs
- Remaining rows: 147 - 28 = 119 rows (each with a unique ID)
- **Distinct external_event_ids:** 14 + 119 = **133**

**Duplicate deliveries = Total rows − Distinct external_event_ids**
**= 147 − 133 = 14**

---

There are **14 duplicate delivery rows** in `channel_callbacks`. These are the extra rows (the 2nd delivery) for the 14 `external_event_id` values that appear twice, meaning each of those 14 events was delivered one extra time.

tasks.finalAnswer:
The routing rule version **2** was in force for payment attempts made on **2025-05-01**.

**Why?** Based on the version validity ranges (`[valid_from, valid_to)`):

| Version | valid_from | valid_to | Covers? |
|---------|------------|----------|---------|
| 1 | 2025-01-01 | 2025-04-01 | Covers up to but **not including** 2025-04-01 |
| 2 | 2025-04-01 | *(NULL = current)* | Covers **2025-04-01 onward** |

Since 2025-05-01 ≥ 2025-04-01, **version 2** (`default-routing` with KCP deprioritized) was active. This is confirmed by the actual routing decisions for attempts on that date, which all reference `rule_version_id: 2`.

| Status | Stars |
|---|---|
| ✅ **Settled** (period 2025-01-01 to 2025-06-01) | **389** |
| ⏳ **Pending** (gift events from 2025-06-01 onward) | **110** |

**Details:**
- **Settled:** One completed settlement covering 2025-01-01 through 2025-06-01 (exclusive), with 389 stars paid out.
- **Pending:** Revenue splits for gift events occurring on or after 2025-06-01 total 110 stars (comprising 22 + 33 + 55 stars from three gift events on June 3rd, 13th, and 22nd).

| Category | Count |
|---|---|

- **6** out of **15** intents succeeded on their first attempt.
- **13** out of **15** intents eventually succeeded (the 6 above plus 7 more that required retries).
- The remaining **2** intents are still in `processing` status.

transcript.content: Now I can see the relevant fields. Let me count both for May 2025.

tasks.finalAnswer:
In **May 2025**:

- **Payments started (payment intents):** 16
- **Individual payment tries (payment attempts):** 23

So there were 23 attempts across 16 intents, meaning about **7 retries** (failed first attempts that led to subsequent tries) happened that month.

tasks.finalAnswer:
## Product 2 Pricing

**Product 2** is a subscription product priced in **USD**.

| Period | Price Version | Unit Price |
|---|---|---|
| **Early February 2025** (valid from Jan 1 to Mar 1, 2025) | Version ID 2 | **$10.00** (1,000 minor units) |
| **Early April 2025** (valid from Mar 1, 2025 onward — current) | Version ID 3 | **$11.00** (1,100 minor units) |

So the price increased from **$10.00 to $11.00** — a $1.00 (10%) rise — effective March 1, 2025. By early February it was still the old price; by early April it had moved to the new price.

tasks.finalAnswer:
I see — the email field is **masked** (shown as `u***@example.com` for all records) and inaccessible for direct filtering. Unfortunately, it's not possible to search for customers by exact email address (e.g., `alice@example.com`) because:

1. The `email` field is **masked** — the system only exposes obfuscated values like `u***@example.com`.
2. The field is also **not filterable** — filtering on `email` returns the error "unknown or inaccessible field."

So the database does not allow querying by raw email address. You would need to look this up through a different system that has access to the unmasked email data.

tasks.finalAnswer:
**5 successful payments have no ledger transaction yet.**

Here's the breakdown of those 5 payment intents (status = `succeeded`) with no matching `ledger_transactions` record:

| Intent ID | Completed At | Amount | Currency |
|-----------|-------------|--------|----------|
| 26 | 2025-02-19 09:01 | 17,500 | KRW |
| 39 | 2025-03-17 09:11 | 10,200 | USD |
| 64 | 2025-05-07 09:01 | 12,700 | CNY |
| 77 | 2025-06-02 09:11 | 3,500 | USD |
| 90 | 2025-06-27 09:11 | 17,900 | KRW |

**Timing caveats:**

- The most recent ledger posting occurred at **2025-06-26 09:30**. Payment intent **#90** (completed 2025-06-27 09:11) was **completed after the last ledger batch ran**, so its absence may simply reflect a normal processing delay (e.g., scheduled ledger posting job hasn't picked it up yet).
- The other **4 intents (#26, #39, #64, #77)** completed between February and early June — significantly earlier — so their absence from the ledger is anomalous and likely indicates a genuine gap (e.g., a failed or skipped posting job).

**Summary:** 75 succeeded payment intents exist, 70 have ledger transactions → **5 do not** (4 likely anomalous, 1 potentially just delayed).

tasks.finalAnswer:
Here's the data broken down by currency. Since these are three different currencies (USD, CNY, KRW) and there are no exchange rates in the database to convert between them, they cannot be meaningfully summed into a single number.

However, here's the breakdown of order totals:

| Currency | Scale | Orders | Total (minor units) | Total (display units) |
|----------|-------|-------:|--------------------:|----------------------:|
| **USD**  | 2 decimals | 48 | 486,600 | **$4,866.00** |
| **CNY**  | 2 decimals | 48 | 486,200 | **¥4,862.00** |
| **KRW**  | 0 decimals | 24 | 243,200 | **₩243,200** |

Without a currency exchange rate table, I cannot convert these into a single combined number. They are three separate currencies with different values. If you could provide exchange rates (or if such data exists elsewhere), I could compute a combined total in a base currency of your choice.

content.text: [{"description":"Currencies; minor_unit_scale is the exponent between the minor unit stored in *_minor columns and the display unit (2 means 100 minor = 1 unit, 0 means minor = unit)","name":"currencies"},{"description":"Countries and their sales region","name":"countries"},{"description":"Tenant organizations (business owners of applications)","name":"organizations"},{"description":"Applications (independently operated products under an organization); currency_code is the settlement currency of all money amounts of that application","name":"applications"},{"description":"Platform login accounts (staff and operators); NOT the transacting customers — see the customers entity","name":"users"},{"description":"Transacting customers of an organization; deleted_at marks logical deletion (excluded from active-customer counts)","name":"customers"},{"description":"Sellable products; current price lives in product_price_versions (never on this table)","name":"products"},{"description":"Versioned product prices in minor units; a version is valid for order dates in [valid_from, valid_to), open-ended when valid_to is NULL. Historical orders keep the price valid at order creation","name":"product_price_versions"},{"description":"Business orders (transaction intent, NOT payment success); money in minor units of currency_code. created_at / confirmed_at / cancelled_at / completed_at are distinct lifecycle times","name":"orders"},{"description":"Order line items (one row per product in an order — finer grain than orders); unit_price_minor is the historical price at order creation","name":"order_items"},{"description":"External payment channels (PSPs)","name":"payment_channels"},{"description":"Versioned routing rules; a version applies to attempts whose created time falls in [valid_from, valid_to). Historical queries must use the version valid at attempt time","name":"routing_rule_versions"},{"description":"Payment intents: one business payment goal per order (NOT a single channel request — see payment_attempts). Intent success rate and attempt success rate are different metrics","name":"payment_intents"},{"description":"Individual payment attempts: one intent can have several (failed attempts precede a retry on another channel). First-attempt result and final intent result differ","name":"payment_attempts"},{"description":"Routing decision per attempt: candidates, selected channel, the rule version in force at attempt time, and the reason","name":"routing_decisions"},{"description":"Refunds against succeeded payment intents; partial refunds are common (amount below the intent amount)","name":"refunds"},{"description":"Raw channel callback events: duplicates, delays, and out-of-order delivery all occur. external_event_id identifies the channel-side event; rows sharing it are duplicate deliveries. occurred_at (channel), received_at (gateway), processed_at (internal, NULL = received but not processed) are distinct times","name":"channel_callbacks"},{"description":"Internal ledger accounts (platform revenue, channel fee expense, merchant payable pools)","name":"ledger_accounts"},{"description":"Versioned channel fee rules in basis points; a version applies to payments whose success time falls in [valid_from, valid_to). Recomputing history must use the version valid at payment time","name":"fee_rule_versions"},{"description":"Ledger transactions (immutable posting units); entries of one transaction share its ID and balance to zero. A succeeded payment without a posted transaction has not reached the ledger yet","name":"ledger_transactions"},{"description":"Ledger entries: one debit/credit per row in minor units; direction is debit or credit; a transaction's entries sum to zero per currency","name":"ledger_entries"},{"description":"Settlements paid out to organizations; net_minor = gross - fees - refunds for the covered period. Settled is distinct from posted and from bank arrival","name":"settlements"},{"description":"Line items from channel-issued statements (external side of reconciliation); amounts and references come from the channel and can disagree with internal records","name":"external_statement_items"},{"description":"Reconciliation outcome per succeeded payment: matched, amount_mismatch, missing_external (internal success without a statement line), or missing_internal (statement line without internal record)","name":"reconciliation_results"},{"description":"Month-end account balance snapshots in minor units (a snapshot is a point-in-time fact, not a flow; recomputing from ledger_entries must agree)","name":"balance_snapshots"},{"description":"Creator agencies (guilds) taking a revenue share from signed creators","name":"agencies"},{"description":"Live streamers; agency_id NULL means independent (no agency share)","name":"creators"},{"description":"Long-lived live rooms (a room hosts many sessions; room lifetime income != single-session income)","name":"live_rooms"},{"description":"Individual broadcast sessions of a room (session grain is finer than room grain)","name":"live_sessions"},{"description":"Gift catalog; star prices are versioned in gift_price_versions (historical events keep the price at event time)","name":"gift_definitions"},{"description":"Versioned gift prices in stars (virtual asset, not fiat); valid for event times in [valid_from, valid_to), NULL valid_to = current","name":"gift_price_versions"},{"description":"Versioned revenue share rules in basis points; splits must use the version valid at gift event time","name":"revenue_share_rule_versions"},{"description":"Gift-sending events in sessions; NOT 1:1 with charges — charge_failed events consumed nothing, reversed events were charged then reversed, duplicate rows (dedup_of set) are repeat deliveries and must not be double counted","name":"gift_events"},{"description":"Revenue split per processed gift event in stars, using the share rule version valid at event time; platform_stars = total - creator - agency","name":"revenue_splits"},{"description":"Completed creator settlements in stars for a covered period; creator income after the period end is pending and NOT included","name":"creator_settlements"},{"description":"Customers visible to the current tenant only; organization 1 is the diagnostic analyst's tenant","name":"tenant_customers"}]

---

These are unique external events with no duplicate delivery. Example records:

| external_event_id | id | attempt_id | channel_id | event_type | occurred_at | processed_at |

| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

*(119 external_event_ids total with count=1)*

---

## Groups with 2 callbacks (duplicate deliveries) — **14 groups**

These are external events where the channel sent the same event twice (rows sharing `external_event_id` are duplicate deliveries):

| # | external_event_id | Callback IDs | attempt_id | channel_id | event_type | Duplicate details |

| 7 | **evt-adyen-000063** | 69, 70 | 63 | 2 | payment_failed | Same occurred_at; first has `processed_at=NULL` (received but unprocessed), second processed at 09:04:35 |
| 8 | **evt-kcp-000072** | 79, 80 | 72 | 5 | payment_processing | Same occurred_at, different received_at (09:01:30 vs 09:04:30) |
| 9 | **evt-alipay-000081** | 89, 90 | 81 | 3 | payment_processing | Same occurred_at, different received_at (09:01:30 vs 09:04:30) |
| 10 | **evt-stripe-000090** | 99, 100 | 90 | 1 | payment_failed | Same occurred_at, different received_at (09:01:30 vs 09:04:30) |
| 11 | **evt-kcp-000099** | 109, 110 | 99 | 5 | payment_failed | Same timestamps (identical occasion — both at 15:00:30/15:00:35) |
| 12 | **evt-alipay-000108** | 119, 120 | 108 | 3 | payment_failed | Same occurred_at, different received_at (09:01:30 vs 09:04:30) |
| 13 | **evt-stripe-000117** | 129, 130 | 117 | 1 | payment_failed | Same occurred_at, different received_at (09:11:30 vs 09:14:30) |
| 14 | **evt-wechatpay-000126** | 139, 140 | 126 | 4 | payment_failed | Same occurred_at, different received_at (09:01:30 vs 09:04:30) |

**Summary:** 147 total callbacks → **133 unique external events** (119 singletons + 14 duplicate groups). The duplicates are all cases where the channel redelivered the same event, typically received seconds-to-minutes apart by the gateway.

content.text: [{"description":"Currencies; minor_unit_scale is the exponent between the minor unit stored in *_minor columns and the display unit (2 means 100 minor = 1 unit, 0 means minor = unit)","name":"currencies"},{"description":"Countries and their sales region","name":"countries"},{"description":"Tenant organizations (business owners of applications)","name":"organizations"},{"description":"Applications (independently operated products under an organization); currency_code is the settlement currency of all money amounts of that application","name":"applications"},{"description":"Platform login accounts (staff and operators); NOT the transacting customers — see the customers entity","name":"users"},{"description":"Transacting customers of an organization; deleted_at marks logical deletion (excluded from active-customer counts)","name":"customers"},{"description":"Sellable products; current price lives in product_price_versions (never on this table)","name":"products"},{"description":"Versioned product prices in minor units; a version is valid for order dates in [valid_from, valid_to), open-ended when valid_to is NULL. Historical orders keep the price valid at order creation","name":"product_price_versions"},{"description":"Business orders (transaction intent, NOT payment success); money in minor units of currency_code. created_at / confirmed_at / cancelled_at / completed_at are distinct lifecycle times","name":"orders"},{"description":"Order line items (one row per product in an order — finer grain than orders); unit_price_minor is the historical price at order creation","name":"order_items"},{"description":"External payment channels (PSPs)","name":"payment_channels"},{"description":"Versioned routing rules; a version applies to attempts whose created time falls in [valid_from, valid_to). Historical queries must use the version valid at attempt time","name":"routing_rule_versions"},{"description":"Payment intents: one business payment goal per order (NOT a single channel request — see payment_attempts). Intent success rate and attempt success rate are different metrics","name":"payment_intents"},{"description":"Individual payment attempts: one intent can have several (failed attempts precede a retry on another channel). First-attempt result and final intent result differ","name":"payment_attempts"},{"description":"Routing decision per attempt: candidates, selected channel, the rule version in force at attempt time, and the reason","name":"routing_decisions"},{"description":"Refunds against succeeded payment intents; partial refunds are common (amount below the intent amount)","name":"refunds"},{"description":"Raw channel callback events: duplicates, delays, and out-of-order delivery all occur. external_event_id identifies the channel-side event; rows sharing it are duplicate deliveries. occurred_at (channel), received_at (gateway), processed_at (internal, NULL = received but not processed) are distinct times","name":"channel_callbacks"},{"description":"Internal ledger accounts (platform revenue, channel fee expense, merchant payable pools)","name":"ledger_accounts"},{"description":"Versioned channel fee rules in basis points; a version applies to payments whose success time falls in [valid_from, valid_to). Recomputing history must use the version valid at payment time","name":"fee_rule_versions"},{"description":"Ledger transactions (immutable posting units); entries of one transaction share its ID and balance to zero. A succeeded payment without a posted transaction has not reached the ledger yet","name":"ledger_transactions"},{"description":"Ledger entries: one debit/credit per row in minor units; direction is debit or credit; a transaction's entries sum to zero per currency","name":"ledger_entries"},{"description":"Settlements paid out to organizations; net_minor = gross - fees - refunds for the covered period. Settled is distinct from posted and from bank arrival","name":"settlements"},{"description":"Line items from channel-issued statements (external side of reconciliation); amounts and references come from the channel and can disagree with internal records","name":"external_statement_items"},{"description":"Reconciliation outcome per succeeded payment: matched, amount_mismatch, missing_external (internal success without a statement line), or missing_internal (statement line without internal record)","name":"reconciliation_results"},{"description":"Month-end account balance snapshots in minor units (a snapshot is a point-in-time fact, not a flow; recomputing from ledger_entries must agree)","name":"balance_snapshots"},{"description":"Creator agencies (guilds) taking a revenue share from signed creators","name":"agencies"},{"description":"Live streamers; agency_id NULL means independent (no agency share)","name":"creators"},{"description":"Long-lived live rooms (a room hosts many sessions; room lifetime income != single-session income)","name":"live_rooms"},{"description":"Individual broadcast sessions of a room (session grain is finer than room grain)","name":"live_sessions"},{"description":"Gift catalog; star prices are versioned in gift_price_versions (historical events keep the price at event time)","name":"gift_definitions"},{"description":"Versioned gift prices in stars (virtual asset, not fiat); valid for event times in [valid_from, valid_to), NULL valid_to = current","name":"gift_price_versions"},{"description":"Versioned revenue share rules in basis points; splits must use the version valid at gift event time","name":"revenue_share_rule_versions"},{"description":"Gift-sending events in sessions; NOT 1:1 with charges — charge_failed events consumed nothing, reversed events were charged then reversed, duplicate rows (dedup_of set) are repeat deliveries and must not be double counted","name":"gift_events"},{"description":"Revenue split per processed gift event in stars, using the share rule version valid at event time; platform_stars = total - creator - agency","name":"revenue_splits"},{"description":"Completed creator settlements in stars for a covered period; creator income after the period end is pending and NOT included","name":"creator_settlements"},{"description":"Customers visible to the current tenant only; organization 1 is the diagnostic analyst's tenant","name":"tenant_customers"}]
